title: Read and write text files
instruction:
`File.WriteAllText` and `File.ReadAllText` are the simplest file operations.

Run this.
successMessage: WriteAllText creates or overwrites. ReadAllText reads the entire file. Simple and direct for small files.

title: Read lines
instruction:
`File.ReadAllLines` returns a string array.
`File.ReadLines` is lazy — loads one line at a time.

Run this.
successMessage: WriteAllLines writes each element on its own line. ReadAllLines loads all lines into an array. Use ReadLines for very large files.

title: Append to a file
instruction:
`File.AppendAllText` adds text to an existing file.

Run this.
successMessage: AppendAllText preserves existing content. WriteAllText would overwrite it.

title: StreamWriter for large files
instruction:
Use `StreamWriter` when writing many lines or large data.

Run this.
successMessage: using automatically closes (disposes) the StreamWriter. This flushes the buffer and releases the file handle.

title: StreamReader for large files
instruction:
Read large files line by line with `StreamReader`.

Run this.
successMessage: StreamReader reads line by line — memory efficient for huge files. The using block ensures the file is closed even if an exception occurs.

title: Path class
instruction:
`Path` helps build and parse file paths in a cross-platform way.

Run this.
successMessage: Path.Combine uses the OS separator. GetFileName, GetExtension, GetDirectoryName parse path components.

title: Directory operations
instruction:
`Directory` creates and lists directories.

Run this.
successMessage: CreateDirectory is idempotent — no error if it already exists. GetFiles matches a pattern. Delete with recursive=true removes the directory and all contents.

title: File.Exists and metadata
instruction:
Check if a file exists and read its metadata.

Run this.
successMessage: FileInfo gives rich metadata. File.Exists for quick checks, FileInfo for detailed information like creation time, last modified, etc.
